steps.aiPrompt:
Analyze the file `vitest`. Based on its incoming connections, it acts as a coupling bottleneck. Please split this file into the following smaller modules:
- `vitest_unit.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: utils.test.js, transformResponse.test.js, toFormData.test.js, regression.test.js, prototypePollution.test.js, platform.test.js, parseProtocol.test.js, fromDataURI.test.js, estimateDataURLDecodedBytes.test.js, composeSignals.test.js, axiosHeaders.test.js, axios.test.js, api.test.js

- `vitest_core.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: transformData.test.js, mergeConfig.test.js, buildFullPath.test.js, AxiosError.test.js
- `vitest_cancel.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: isCancel.test.js, canceledError.test.js
- `vitest_adapters.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: http.test.js, fetch.test.js, errorDetails.test.js, adapters.test.js
- `vitest_tests.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: urlencode.smoke.test.js, timeout.smoke.test.js, rateLimit.smoke.test.js, progress.smoke.test.js, interceptors.smoke.test.js, instance.smoke.test.js, import.smoke.test.js, http2.smoke.test.js, headers.smoke.test.js, formData.smoke.test.js, files.smoke.test.js, fetch.smoke.test.js, error.smoke.test.js, cancel.smoke.test.js, basic.smoke.test.js, auth.smoke.test.js, typings.module.test.js, ts.module.test.js, fixture-cleanup.module.test.js
- `vitest_setup.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: browser.setup.js
- `vitest_browser.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: xsrf.browser.test.js, transform.browser.test.js, toFormData.browser.test.js, settle.browser.test.js, requests.browser.test.js, promise.browser.test.js, progress.browser.test.js, options.browser.test.js, isURLSameOrigin.browser.test.js, interceptors.browser.test.js, instance.browser.test.js, headers.browser.test.js, formdata.browser.test.js, defaults.browser.test.js, cookies.browser.test.js, cancelToken.browser.test.js, cancel.browser.test.js, basicAuth.browser.test.js, adapter.browser.test.js

steps.aiPrompt:
Analyze the file `axios`. Based on its incoming connections, it acts as a coupling bottleneck. Please split this file into the following smaller modules:
- `axios_tests.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: urlencode.smoke.test.js, timeout.smoke.test.js, rateLimit.smoke.test.js, progress.smoke.test.js, interceptors.smoke.test.js, instance.smoke.test.js, import.smoke.test.js, http2.smoke.test.js, headers.smoke.test.js, formData.smoke.test.js, files.smoke.test.js, fetch.smoke.test.js, error.smoke.test.js, cancel.smoke.test.js, basic.smoke.test.js, auth.smoke.test.js
- `axios_helpers.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: esm-index.ts, esm-functions.ts, ts-require.ts, ts-require-default.ts
- `axios_examples.py`: Extract functionality specific to these dependents: network_enhanced.js

After splitting, securely and automatically update all 21 dependent files to import from the new specific modules instead of the monolithic `axios.py`. DO NOT remove any functionality, only move it.

steps.title: Reduce cross-boundary imports: http.js
steps.description: `lib/adapters/http.js` imports from 8 different modules: `lib`, `lib/core`, `lib/helpers`, `.`, `lib/env`, `lib/defaults`, `lib/cancel`, `lib/platform`. Consider using a facade or dependency injection.
steps.rationale: Files with imports scattered across many modules have high afferent coupling. A facade centralizes these dependencies, making the file easier to test (mock one facade) and reducing the impact of changes in dependent modules.

operations.description: Create dependency facade `http_deps.js` — centralizes 8 cross-module imports
Refactor `http.js` to import from local facade instead of 8 different modules

steps.aiPrompt:
Analyze the file `lib/adapters/http.js` which currently imports from 8 cross-boundary distinct directories/modules (lib, lib/core, lib/helpers, ., lib/env, lib/defaults, lib/cancel, lib/platform).
Please refactor this to use the Dependency Injection (DI) or Facade pattern:
1. Extract these imports into a dedicated file `lib/adapters/http_deps.js`.
2. Re-export or bundle them appropriately.
3. Update `lib/adapters/http.js` to import strictly from the new `lib/adapters/http_deps.js` rather than directly reaching into multiple disjointed modules, lowering its afferent coupling.

steps.rationale: Files with imports scattered across many modules have high afferent coupling. A facade centralizes these dependencies, making the file easier to test (mock one facade) and reducing the impact of changes in dependent modules.

steps.title: Optimize barrel: lib/defaults/index.js
steps.description: `lib/defaults/index.js` re-exports 7 modules. This creates a "Shotgun Surgery" risk — any change propagates widely.
steps.rationale: Barrel files that re-export everything make it hard to tree-shake unused code and create implicit dependencies. Direct imports make dependency relationships explicit and reduce the blast radius of changes.

operations.description: Replace barrel import from `index.js` with direct imports: utils.js, AxiosError.js, toFormData.js, formDataToJSON.js
Replace barrel import from `index.js` with direct imports: utils.js

steps.aiPrompt:
Analyze the barrel file `lib/defaults/index.js` which re-exports 7 modules. This is creating a "Shotgun Surgery" risk.
Please optimize it by:
1. Removing wildcard exports (e.g., `export *`) if any.
2. Only re-exporting the public API interfaces/classes meant for consumers outside this module.
3. Updating consumers (transformResponse.test.js, mergeConfig.test.js, defaults.browser.test.js, axios.js, transformData.js, dispatchRequest.js) to import from the specific files directly instead of passing through this barrel file where appropriate.

steps.title: Group co-dependent files into `shared/`
steps.description: Files `vitest`, `index.js`, `assert`, `axios`, `stream`, `utils.js`, `events`, `AxiosError.js`, `form-data`, `index.js`, `http`, `CanceledError.js`, `node:path`, `fixture.js` are frequently imported together (co-import score: 114). Grouping them improves cohesion.
steps.rationale: Files that are frequently imported together belong in the same module. This improves discoverability and reduces the cognitive load of understanding which files work together.

operations.description: Create new module `shared/` to group 14 co-dependent files
operations.content: """Module shared — grouped by co-import pattern."""

steps.aiPrompt:
Analyze the files: `vitest`, `index.js`, `assert`, `axios`, `stream`, `lib/utils.js`, `events`, `lib/core/AxiosError.js`, `form-data`, `lib/defaults/index.js`, `http`, `lib/cancel/CanceledError.js`, `node:path`, `tests/module/esm/tests/helpers/fixture.js`.
These files are frequently imported together but are currently scattered across different directories.
Please refactor to improve cohesion:
1. Create a new directory named `./shared`.
2. Move these files solidly into this new directory.
3. Add an index/barrel file if appropriate.
4. Securely scan the entire project to fix all broken imports resulting from this move.
